chief_complaint: A 45-year-old female patient presented with sudden onset shortness of breath and chest pain.
history: The patient had a history of chronic thromboembolic pulmonary hypertension (CTEPH) and developed a massive acute-on-chronic pulmonary thromboembolism leading to cardiogenic shock. She was not a candidate for immediate definitive treatment with pulmonary endarterectomy (PEA) due to her critical condition.
exam: Vital signs: BP 80/40 mmHg, HR 120 bpm, RR 30 breaths/min. Physical examination revealed signs of cardiogenic shock.
labs: ECG: showing signs of right heart strain. Bloods: elevated troponin, D-dimer positive. Imaging: CT pulmonary angiogram showing massive pulmonary embolism.
differentials: Cardiogenic shock due to myocardial infarction
Pulmonary embolism with right heart strain
Acute exacerbation of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD)
correct_ranking: Cardiogenic shock due to myocardial infarction
Pulmonary embolism with right heart strain
Acute exacerbation of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD)
